details: Launched in 1976-77, the "Post Matric College Scholarship" is a scheme by the Scheduled Caste Welfare Department, Government of Madhya Pradesh. The scheme provides financial assistance to encourage Scheduled Caste students to pursue higher education. One-third of the subsistence allowance is payable to students residing in departmental hostels who are provided free lodging and boarding facilities.
benefits: Post-matric students are provided with scholarships.
eligibility: The applicant must be a student.
The student should belong to the Scheduled Caste category.
The student must come from a family with an income limit of up to ₹6.00 lakh to qualify.
One-third of the subsistence allowance will be payable to students residing in departmental hostels who are provided with free lodging/boarding facilities.

online: Scroll down the page and find the "Student Corner" section on the left side of the screen.
Under the Student Corner, click on the “Student Registration” option.
Complete the online registration form by filling in all mandatory fields and uploading the required documents in the specified format and size.
Carefully review the details entered and the documents uploaded. Once you have verified everything, click on the "Submit" button.
After submitting, you will receive your registration number on the registered email address or mobile number.
Scroll down to the "Student Corner" section on the left and click on "Student Login." Enter your User ID and Password to log in.
After logging in, a dashboard will appear in front of you. From your dashboard, select the scheme you wish to apply for and click on it.
Complete the application form by filling in all required fields and uploading necessary documents in the specified format and size.
Double-check the information and documents provided, then click on the "Submit" button to submit your application.

faqs.question: What is the "Post Matric College Scholarship" scheme?
faqs.answer: The scheme provides financial assistance to Scheduled Caste students for pursuing higher education.

faqs.question: Who is eligible for this scholarship?
faqs.answer: The student must belong to the Scheduled Caste category and come from a family with an income limit of up to ₹6.00 lakh.

faqs.question: What is the income limit for eligibility?
faqs.answer: The family's income must be up to ₹6.00 lakh per year.

faqs.question: Can students from non-government institutions apply?
faqs.answer: Yes, but students whose parents’ income exceeds ₹2.50 lakh are not eligible for subsistence allowance.

faqs.question: What amount is reimbursed under this scheme?
faqs.answer: Students can get reimbursement for tuition fees, examination fees, and non-refundable compulsory fees.

faqs.question: Are government school students eligible for fee reimbursement?
faqs.answer: Government school students are not eligible for fee reimbursement if their family income exceeds ₹2.50 lakh.

faqs.question: Can students living in departmental hostels apply?
faqs.answer: Yes, students living in departmental hostels can apply, but they will receive one-third of the subsistence allowance if they get free lodging/boarding.

faqs.question: Is there a hostel facility provided under this scheme?
faqs.answer: Yes, students residing in departmental hostels may be eligible for one-third of the subsistence allowance.

faqs.question: Are students from Sambal Yojana families eligible for this scheme?
faqs.answer: No, students from Sambal Yojana families do not have to pay fees at government schools and are not eligible for fee reimbursement.

faqs.question: Do students need to submit any documents for this scheme?
faqs.answer: Yes, students need to submit necessary documents like income certificates, caste certificates, and other required documents.
